title: Fix first-time admin registration unauthorized error

requirements.text: Fix the chicken-and-egg authorization bug in the backend where registering the very first admin fails with 'Unauthorized: Only admins can assign user roles'. The `registerFirstAdmin` function (or equivalent initial admin setup function) must bypass the admin-only auth guard when no admins exist yet in the system. Add a condition: if the admin user count is zero, allow the registration to proceed without requiring an existing admin caller. If one or more admins already exist, the normal admin-only guard must apply.

quotes: I am doing to register admin id its coming Unauthorized: Only admins can assign user roles why ?

acceptanceCriteria: When no admins exist in the system, calling the first-admin registration endpoint succeeds without any authorization error.
The registered principal is stored as an admin and subsequent registrations require an existing admin caller.
When at least one admin exists, calling the registration endpoint without admin privileges returns an authorization error as expected.
The frontend AdminRegistrationPage can complete the registration flow end-to-end without receiving an Unauthorized error on a fresh deployment.

constraints: Do not remove or weaken admin authorization guards for any function other than the first-admin bootstrap path.
The fix must be conditional on zero admins existing — never permanently disable auth checks.
nonGoals: Changing the admin login flow or session management.
Modifying frontend registration form UI or validation.
Adding new admin roles or permissions.
